user_query:
Stress-test my proposal for an internal cross-service notifications platform. Read input/plan.md first. Then run a rigorous, five-phase structured interview: kick-off, job mapping, constraints/feasibility, risk analysis, and synthesis. Ask one question at a time and include your recommended answer to every question so I can accept/reject/refine. Don’t move to a new topic until the current branch is resolved or explicitly parked. After completing the interview and producing a validated synthesis, generate a complete set of implementation-ready specification artifacts. Write all outputs to the following paths:

- output/questions.jsonl — log of the questions you asked (phases 0–3 only), one JSON object per line with keys: phase ("0"–"3"), question (string), recommended_answer (string). Each question must be exactly one question (don’t stack multiple questions in one line).
- output/phase_summaries.md — per-phase decision summaries for phases 0–3, and what’s parked.
- output/synthesis.md — the final structured synthesis covering decisions, open items and how they’ll be resolved, conflicts/tensions, risks with mitigations, and success criteria.
- output/memory/constitution.md — the project’s non-negotiable principles and tradeoff rules.
- output/specs/notification-feature/spec.md — the feature specification. Include at least two user scenarios with Given/When/Then acceptance criteria. You may include “[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]” tags for unresolved items, capped at 3 total in this file.
- output/specs/notification-feature/data-model.md — entities, key fields, relationships, state transitions, and validation rules.
- output/specs/notification-feature/contracts/ — one or more .md files defining API/event/CLI contracts (request/response or event schemas, errors).
- output/specs/notification-feature/tasks.md — dependency-ordered task breakdown; include at least two tasks referencing user stories like [US1], [US2].
- output/specs/notification-feature/checklists/requirements.md — validation checklist derived from acceptance criteria.

Make sure questions.jsonl includes at least one question for each of phases 0, 1, 2, and 3, and at least 8 questions total across those phases. Define measurable success criteria and explicit scope boundaries (in/out), include triple-constraint tradeoffs, and run a pre-mortem plus at least one Five Whys chain. Cross-check that tasks map to user stories and keep “[NEEDS CLARIFICATION]” tags at or below the cap.
